chief_complaint: A 65-year-old woman presented with atypical chest pain and exertional dyspnea.
history: The patient has a history of tongue carcinoma and pulmonary embolism managed with an IVC filter. She underwent imaging tests after an ECG and echocardiography revealed right heart strain.
exam: ECG and echocardiography revealed right heart strain.
labs: Coronary angiography showed 80% stenosis in the mid-segment of the left anterior descending (LAD) artery. Fluoroscopy revealed a migrating IVC filter in the right pulmonary artery and right ventricular outflow tract.
correct_diagnosis: Intracardiopulmonary migration of an inferior vena cava filter and concurrent coronary artery disease
differentials: Intracardiopulmonary migration of an inferior vena cava filter and concurrent coronary artery disease
correct_ranking: Intracardiopulmonary migration of an inferior vena cava filter and concurrent coronary artery disease
